title: clermontia coerulea subsp. brevidens
intro: Clermontia coerulea subsp. brevidens, also known as the blue glory flower or ma'o hau hele, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Clermontia coerulea subsp. brevidens include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically used to make teas, infusions, or topical applications, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
